steps.title: Set up the comparison
steps.content: A data point's actual $y$-value is greater than the predicted $y$-value exactly when the point lies strictly above the line of best fit. So the question reduces to counting points above the line.

steps.title: Count points above the line of best fit
steps.content:
Examine each of the $10$ data points and check whether it sits above the line in the scatterplot. Doing this carefully gives $6$ points above the line.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
